title: Opening Inventory Correction Vs Continuation
focus_questions: Was the first opening drive correction or true continuation?
Did the opening burst actually hold acceptance?

summary.thesis: The open often decides whether overnight inventory is corrected first or continued further, and the first burst is frequently misread.
summary.operator_translation: Do not chase the open until you know whether it is unwinding inventory or truly extending it.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when open location, overnight inventory, and first-drive quality are central to the replay.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: U.S. open with meaningful overnight or prior-session inventory context. Required evidence: open location relative to prior value, first-drive quality, and whether correction or continuation is accepted. Do not trade: by chasing the first burst before its purpose is clear. Real trigger: accepted opening correction or accepted opening continuation after hold quality is proven. Failure point: the first drive quickly loses acceptance and flips the script.

when_relevant: The U.S. open occurs after a directional overnight or prior-session inventory build.
The operator needs to know whether the first opening move is correction or continuation.
Opening location versus prior value and overnight range matters to the next decision.
required_evidence: meaningful overnight or prior-session directional inventory exists
the open can be placed relative to prior close, value, and overnight range
the first opening drive and its hold quality can be reviewed
confirmation_signals: the open clearly corrects back toward old value or clearly accepts further in the prior direction
the first opening drive produces follow-through consistent with correction or continuation rather than random spike behavior
later trade confirms that the first opening move was inventory logic, not just opening noise
invalidation_signals: the first drive immediately loses hold quality and flips
the open is being read without reference to prior value or overnight positioning
correction or continuation is being assumed before acceptance is visible
no_trade_conditions: Do not chase the first opening burst before determining whether it is correction or true continuation.
Do not call correction complete if price has not yet shown acceptance back into old value or back through the correcting path.
Do not assume continuation is real if the first drive extends but immediately loses hold quality.

entry_archetypes.name: accepted opening correction
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the open clearly unwinds inventory and price accepts the correction path.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if correction is only a sharp flush with no acceptance.

entry_archetypes.name: held opening continuation
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the opening drive proves it is real continuation by holding outside and building there.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if continuation is only emotional opening flow.

management_notes: Treat the open as an inventory decision point rather than an automatic signal.
If the first drive fails, the opposite opening script often becomes stronger than the first burst.
Once correction is complete and accepted, shift attention from opening emotion to session structure.
review_questions: Was the operator trading real opening continuation or only an overnight inventory unwind?
Did the first drive hold acceptance, or did it fail after the first emotional burst?
Was the open interpreted in relation to prior value and overnight positioning, or in isolation?
